summary: Fair soundboard recording of an uneven 1989 show, highlighted by Jack‑a‑Roe, Stella Blue and a standout Black Muddy River.
review: The patched SBD source is unbalanced and not fully leveled, making the audio only fair, but the performance itself offers several bright spots. Highlights include an arena‑captivating Stella Blue, a powerful Jack‑a‑Roe that lifts the first set, and what may be the year’s best Black Muddy River. The second half improves after the Drums‑>Space segment, though the show overall feels mixed.

ai_rating.rationale: One detailed review gives a mixed 3‑star overall rating; the musical performance is average‑to‑good with strong moments, while the recording quality is only fair.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Tobin's patched and re‑pitched SBD is not balanced or level‑matched like the official release; reviewers suggest the tobin_125281 matrix version has a better mix, while other audience recordings run slow.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set starts shaky (Greatest Story) but gains momentum with Jack‑a‑Roe and solid mid‑set numbers; second set opens with a controversial Blow Away, dips on Estimated and Crazy Fingers, then lifts after Drums>Space into strong performances.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Arena was hanging on his playing during Stella Blue; he teases GDtRFB and leads the high‑energy moments.
band_member_comments.Phil: Delivers an above‑average bass performance on Tom Thumb's.
band_member_comments.Bob: Brent’s keyboard work is histrionic on Estimated, feeble on Crazy Fingers, but shines on Black Muddy River, making it a year‑standout.
band_member_comments.Keys: Same as Bob – mixed on early second‑set tunes, strong on Black Muddy River.
band_member_comments.Drums: The Drums>Space segment turns the show around, setting up the stronger second‑half material.
